metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_3648
metadata.comment:
Rationale: C1qrl1 promotes climbing fiber synapse refinement during development (Fig. 2) and its interaction with Bai3 is essential for the maintenance of CF synapse in adults (Fig. 5)

28/05/2018 Pim
From ticket #3054: I think that the authors conclude that C1QRF_MOUSE, C1q-related factor, C1qrl1, C1q/TNF-related protein 14 is involved in both maturation of active synapses and the elimination of inactive ones: "These results indicate that C1ql1 has dual functions: strengthening existing CF synapses and inducing the elimination of inactive synapses (see Discussion)". So would it be an idea to create two annotations to terms:
- 'regulation of synapse maturation (GO:0090128)'
- 'maintenance of synapse structure (term to be created)' (Fig.5)
We have changed this ticket to "regulation of synapse maturation" and we agree to generate an additional with the new term you suggested.

29/05/2018 Pim
- Since the interaction of C1QRF with Bia3 can induce the elimination of synapses I decided to not annotate to 'maintenance of synapse structure (GO:0099558)' but to "regulation of synapse disassembly".. Experimental description: Here, we show that C1ql1, a member of the C1q family of proteins, is provided by climbing fibers (CFs) and serves as a crucial anterograde signal to determine and maintain the single-winner CF in the mouse cerebellum throughout development and adulthood. Abstract, p. 316
